caveats: **[MEDIUM] Correctness — `fetch_recent_commits` sha always empty** (server.py:958): `"sha": str(commit.get("sha", ""))[:8]` reads `sha` from the nested `commit` object, but the GitHub `/commits` API places `sha` at the top level of each item (`item["sha"]`), not inside `item["commit"]`. `item["commit"]` has no `sha` key, so this always yields `""`. Fix: read `str(item.get("sha", ""))[:8]` (from the outer `item`, guarded by the existing `isinstance(item, dict)` check) instead of from `commit`. No

**[LOW/INFO] Edge case — future `pushed_at`** (server.py:976): a `pushed_at` in the future yields a negative `delta.days`, which satisfies `delta.days < 7` → classified "active". Harmless (no crash, no wrong data exposure) but semantically a future-dated repo is treated as active. Optional hardening: clamp negative deltas or treat `delta.total_seconds() < 0` as "stalled".
